server.name: Manager AI
tool.get_issue_details.description:
Get all details of a specific issue. Returns: id, project_id, name, description, status, priority, specification (markdown), plan (markdown), recap (markdown), tasks (atomic plan tasks), created_at, updated_at.

Issue state machine: NEW → REASONING → PLANNED → ACCEPTED → FINISHED (or CANCELED from any state).
tool.get_issue_status.description:
Get the current status of an issue.

Issue state machine: NEW → REASONING → PLANNED → ACCEPTED → FINISHED (or CANCELED from any state).
tool.get_project_context.description: Get project information (name, path, description, tech_stack).
tool.set_issue_name.description: Set the name of an issue after analysis. Can be called at any point to give the issue a meaningful title.
tool.complete_issue.description:
Mark an issue as Finished and save the recap. Only works for issues in Accepted status. All tasks must be completed first.

This is the final step in the workflow: create_issue_spec → create_issue_plan → create_plan_tasks → accept_issue → (implement & update tasks) → complete_issue.
tool.create_issue_spec.description:
Write a specification for an issue and move it to Reasoning status. Only works for issues in New status.

This is the first step in the issue workflow. After writing the spec, use create_issue_plan to write the implementation plan.
tool.edit_issue_spec.description: Update the specification of an issue in Reasoning status. Use this to refine the spec before creating a plan.
tool.create_issue_plan.description:
Write an implementation plan for an issue and move it to Planned status. Only works for issues in Reasoning status.

After creating the plan, use create_plan_tasks to define atomic tasks, then the user can accept_issue to begin implementation.
tool.edit_issue_plan.description: Update the implementation plan of an issue in Planned status. Use this to incorporate feedback before the plan is accepted.
tool.accept_issue.description:
Accept an issue's plan and move it to Accepted status. Only works for issues in Planned status.

After acceptance, implement the plan by updating task statuses, then call complete_issue with a recap when done.
tool.cancel_issue.description: Cancel an issue from any status. Use this when the issue is no longer needed or was created in error.
tool.create_plan_tasks.description:
Create atomic tasks for an issue's plan. Accepts a list of task objects with 'name' field. Tasks track implementation progress.

Call this after create_issue_plan to break the plan into trackable steps.
tool.replace_plan_tasks.description: Replace all tasks for an issue. Deletes existing tasks and creates new ones from the provided list. Use this when the plan changes significantly.
tool.update_task_status.description: Update the status of an atomic plan task. Valid transitions: Pending → In Progress → Completed.
tool.update_task_name.description: Update the name of an atomic plan task.
tool.delete_task.description: Delete a single atomic plan task.
tool.get_plan_tasks.description: Get all atomic plan tasks for an issue, ordered by execution order.
tool.send_notification.description:
Send a notification to the user via the Manager AI web interface. Use this to notify the user when:
- You need their input or a decision (e.g., plan is ready for review)
- A significant milestone is reached (e.g., all tasks completed)
- An error or blocker requires their attention

Do NOT use this for routine status updates — update task statuses instead so the user can track progress in the UI.
tool.update_project_context.description: Update the project context (description and/or tech_stack). Only updates fields that are provided.
tool.search_project_context.description: Search project context using semantic similarity. Searches across embedded files and completed issues. Returns a list of matching chunks with preview, score, and source info. Use get_context_chunk_details to get the full text of a result.
tool.get_context_chunk_details.descripti: Get full details of a specific context chunk found via search_project_context. Returns the complete text, metadata, and links to adjacent chunks for navigation.
tool.get_next_issue.description: Get the next workable issue for a project, ordered by priority. Returns an ACCEPTED issue (ready for implementation) first, then a NEW issue (needs planning). Returns null if no workable issues exist. Use this to implement a continuous work queue: after completing an issue, call get_next_issue to find the next one to start.
